title: pulicaria argyrophylla subsp. oligophylla
intro: Pulicaria argyrophylla subsp. oligophylla, also known as the woolly-leaved fleabane, is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, characterized by its distinctive woolly foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pulicaria argyrophylla subsp. oligophylla include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
